description: This pipeline appears to be designed for image and data processing, specifically for analyzing biomedical microscopic images. It begins by detecting spots within an image, then filters these spots based on certain expressions. The image is also processed to merge 2D slices into a hyperstack, which can be controlled via expressions. The pipeline then proceeds to track the detected spots and applies various operations to the tracks, including outputting the results. Additionally, it handles raw data input and performs multiple processing steps to generate a final output. Overall, the pipeline seems to automate a workflow for image analysis, spot detection, tracking, and data processing.

c40e5d98-8142-4c15-be31-ed779b2fc246.name: trackmate-detector
c40e5d98-8142-4c15-be31-ed779b2fc246.description:
Detect spots using TrackMate

This algorithm groups the incoming data based on the annotations. Those groups can consist of one data item per slot.

1065d1bf-9c32-4700-a022-9c59c400d7e0.name: ij1-dimensions-merge-2d-to-hyperstack
1065d1bf-9c32-4700-a022-9c59c400d7e0.description:
Merges all incoming 2D slices into a hyperstack. The slice positions within the hyperstack can be controlled via expressions. The node can handle missing and negative slice positions due to a remapping procedure. Please ensure that there are no duplicate locations.

This algorithm groups the incoming data based on the annotations. Those groups can consist of multiple data items. If you want to group all data into one output, set the matching strategy to 'Custom' and leave 'Data set matching annotations' empty.
